# Coding Guidelines

<!-- Generated by /ai-context. Manual edits are overwritten on re-run. -->

## AS IS â€” Current state

No linter or formatter is configured â€” no `.eslintrc*`, `.prettier*`, `.editorconfig`, pre-commit hooks, or `.github/workflows` found. Patterns below are observed consistently across â‰Ą2 source files, not tool-enforced unless noted.

### 1. Pure domain logic separated from Vue reactivity
`app/services/*.ts` (`csvParser.ts`, `columnStats.ts`, `columnFilters.ts`, `viewerSession.ts`, `exportData.ts`, `exportXlsx.ts`, `formatFile.ts`, `diffDatasets.ts`) contain zero Vue imports â€” plain functions/types only, runnable inside a Web Worker or a plain Vitest test. `viewerSession.ts:1-9` states the convention explicitly ("FunĂ§Ăµes puras, sem import de Vue/composables ... seguindo a convenĂ§ĂŁo de `app/services/`"); `diffDatasets.ts:12` repeats it verbatim ("Framework-free, client-side (`app/services/`...)"). Rationale: `csvParser.worker.ts` reuses `runParseRequest` from `csvParser.ts` directly (`/// <reference lib="webworker" />` + `self.addEventListener`), `orderedColumnIndices` (`csvParser.ts:236-252`) is called from both `useSaveVersion.ts` (serialization) and `useViewer.ts` (display) without either importing the other, and each service ships its own `*.spec.ts` with no component mount required. No enforcement tool; verified by import inspection of `app/services/csvParser.ts`, `app/services/columnStats.ts`, and `app/services/diffDatasets.ts`.

### 2. Module-scope singleton state in composables
Shared state lives as module-level `ref`s outside the composable function, so multiple call sites share one source of truth without a store library. Observed in `useCurrentDataset.ts:36-37` (`const dataset = ref<Dataset | null>(null)`), `useOpenFile.ts:55-57` (`error`, `isOpening`, `progress`), `useTheme.ts:21-22` (`theme`, `initialized`), `useCellEditing.ts:71-79` (`editingCell`, `validationError`, `undoStack`, `redoStack`, `savedPosition` â€” reset via a module-level `watch` on the dataset object itself, `useCellEditing.ts:115-127`), `useComparisonDatasets.ts:50-55` (`datasetB`, `metaB`, `keyColumn`, `comparisonError`, `isOpeningB` â€” reset via a module-level `watch` on `useCurrentDataset().meta.value?.id`, `useComparisonDatasets.ts:74`), and `useUnsavedChangesGuard.ts:18-20` (`isOpen`, `showSaveCopyModal`, `pendingPath` â€” shared between the always-mounted `default.vue` layout and `viewer.vue` "so the layout and the page use the same modal instance without prop-drilling"). Composables expose `readonly()` wrappers over the refs (`useCurrentDataset.ts:55-56`, `useOpenFile.ts:185-187`, `useCellEditing.ts:282-284`, `useComparisonDatasets.ts:201-210`) so consumers cannot mutate state directly.

### 3. Dependency injection via options objects, defaulted for production
Composables that touch IO (Worker, IndexedDB, router) accept an options object where every dependency has a production default, enabling tests to substitute fakes. Observed in `useCsvParser(options: UseCsvParserOptions = {})` (`createWorker` defaults to `defaultCreateWorker`, `useCsvParser.ts:95-96`), `useOpenFile(options: UseOpenFileOptions = {})` (`parser`, `filesStore`, `currentDataset`, `navigate` all defaulted, `useOpenFile.ts:65-70`), `useSaveVersion(options: UseSaveVersionOptions = {})` (`currentDataset`/`filesStore`/`cellEditing` default to `useCurrentDataset()`/`useFilesStore()`/`useCellEditing()`, `useSaveVersion.ts:37-39`), `useViewerSession(viewer, meta, options: UseViewerSessionOptions = {})` (`sessionStore` defaults to `useSessionStore()`, `debounceMs` defaults to `300`, `useViewerSession.ts:66-67`), and `useComparisonDatasets(options: UseComparisonDatasetsOptions = {})` (`parser` defaults to `useCsvParser()`, `filesStore` defaults to `useFilesStore()`, `useComparisonDatasets.ts:77-78`). Rationale: `test/useOpenFile.spec.ts`, `test/useCsvParser.spec.ts`, `test/useSaveVersion.spec.ts`, and `test/useViewerSession.spec.ts` inject deterministic fakes instead of a real Worker/IndexedDB/router.

### 4. `<script setup lang="ts">` SFCs with typed props/emits
Every component uses Composition API `<script setup>` with `defineProps<{...}>()`/`defineEmits<{...}>()` generic type syntax, not runtime prop validators. Observed in `app/components/CsvCell.vue:4-16` (`defineProps<{ value: string | number | null | undefined; numeric?: boolean; selected?: boolean }>()`) and `app/components/ViewerToolbar.vue:24-37`. No enforcement tool; TypeScript compiler would catch mismatches but `vue-tsc` is currently broken on TS7 (user memory `vue-tsc-typescript7-broken.md`) â€” validated via `yarn test` instead.

### 5. Scoped CSS with CSS custom properties, no utility-class-only styling
Components ship a `<style scoped>` block using design-token custom properties (`var(--text)`, `var(--bg-1)`, `var(--accent)`, `var(--border)`) rather than Tailwind utility classes in the template, even though Tailwind v4 is the global CSS entrypoint (`app/assets/css/main.css`, wired in `nuxt.config.ts`). Observed in `CsvCell.vue:41-83`, `StatsPanel.vue:183-394`, `ViewerToolbar.vue:126-266`.

### 6. JSDoc-heavy inline documentation in Portuguese, citing spec IDs
Every service/composable file opens with a JSDoc block explaining responsibility and cites user-story/requirement IDs (`US-1.1`, `RF-04`, `RNF-03`) and a phase reference. Observed in `app/services/csvParser.ts:1-16`, `app/services/columnStats.ts:1-14`, `app/composables/useViewer.ts:12-25`. Not tool-enforced; a stylistic convention held consistently across the domain layer.
